Please figure out what kind of issue this is and tell me the right Spectrum next step.

content.content:
content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Outage Information and Troubleshooting\
  \ | Spectrum Support\n- url: https://www.spectrum.net/support/internet/troubleshooting-your-spectrum-services-after-outage\n\
  - source: Spectrum Support\n- domain: spectrum.net\n\n## Content\n\nOUTAGE INFORMATION\
  \ AND TROUBLESHOOTING\n\nWhen Spectrum Internet service stops working, first determine\
  \ whether the current Spectrum account shows a known service interruption affecting\
  \ the service location. Spectrum directs customers to account-based service information\
  \ through Spectrum.net or the My Spectrum App so that outage information can be\
  \ tied to the customer\u2019s own services and location.\n\nIf the account shows\
  \ that the service is affected by a known outage, use the outage status and restoration\
  \ information associated with that account. A general report about problems elsewhere\
  \ does not establish whether a particular Spectrum service location is included\
  \ in the same interruption.\n\nAfter an outage or a technician visit, equipment\
  \ may still need attention. In Spectrum\u2019s account tools, go to the Services\
  \ area, choose the Internet section, and select the relevant equipment. When a device\
  \ is marked with a connection problem, Spectrum\u2019s troubleshooting flow directs\
  \ the customer to select Troubleshoot. The account tools may then offer an equipment\
  \ restart. Choose Restart Equipment for the affected device and confirm the restart.\n\
  \nIf there is no active outage shown for the service location, or if the known outage\
  \ has ended but the connection problem remains, the remaining issue should be handled\
  \ through the individual equipment and connection troubleshooting flow rather than\
  \ treated as an ongoing area outage. Check the equipment status shown in the account,\
  \ use the troubleshooting controls for the affected device, and continue with connection\
  \ support if the problem is not resolved.\n\nThe outage check and the equipment\
  \ troubleshooting flow answer different questions. Outage information establishes\
  \ whether the current service location is part of a known service interruption.\
  \ Equipment status and troubleshooting address a connection problem associated with\
  \ the customer\u2019s own service and devices.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Outage Information and Troubleshooting | Spectrum Support
url: https://www.spectrum.net/support/internet/troubleshooting-your-spectrum-services-after-outage

args.official_next_step: Use Spectrum account Services > Internet > affected equipment > Troubleshoot; select Restart Equipment, then continue with connection support if unresolved.
args.reason: Reconcile the account, billing, outage, equipment, and connection observations to identify whether the outage is billing-related and determine the correct Spectrum recovery path.

args.official_next_step: Use Spectrum account Services > Internet > affected equipment > Troubleshoot; select Restart Equipment, then continue with connection support if unresolved.
args.reason: Reconcile the account, billing, outage, equipment, and connection observations to identify whether the outage is billing-related and determine the correct Spectrum recovery path.

content.content:
This appears to be a **connection/equipment issue, not a billing-related suspension or area outage**:

- Your Spectrum account is active.
- The **$27 past-due amount is not currently suspending or disconnecting service**.
- No active outage matches your service address.
- Your equipment is reachable, but the internet connection is offline.
- No repair ticket or appointment is currently open.

**Next Spectrum step:**  
Sign in to **Spectrum.net or the My Spectrum App**, then go to:

**Services → Internet → select the affected equipment → Troubleshoot → Restart Equipment**

Confirm the restart and test your connection. If it remains offline, continue through Spectrum’s connection-support flow to request further assistance or arrange repair.

You should still address the **$27 past-due balance**—the account shows a $91.42 current balance, with a partial payment previously received—but it is not the cause of this interruption at present.

Source: [Spectrum Support—Outage Information and Troubleshooting](https://www.spectrum.net/support/internet/troubleshooting-your-spectrum-services-after-outage)

final_answer:
This appears to be a **connection/equipment issue, not a billing-related suspension or area outage**:

- Your Spectrum account is active.
- The **$27 past-due amount is not currently suspending or disconnecting service**.
- No active outage matches your service address.
- Your equipment is reachable, but the internet connection is offline.
- No repair ticket or appointment is currently open.

**Next Spectrum step:**  
Sign in to **Spectrum.net or the My Spectrum App**, then go to:

**Services → Internet → select the affected equipment → Troubleshoot → Restart Equipment**

Confirm the restart and test your connection. If it remains offline, continue through Spectrum’s connection-support flow to request further assistance or arrange repair.

You should still address the **$27 past-due balance**—the account shows a $91.42 current balance, with a partial payment previously received—but it is not the cause of this interruption at present.

Source: [Spectrum Support—Outage Information and Troubleshooting](https://www.spectrum.net/support/internet/troubleshooting-your-spectrum-services-after-outage)
